hypothesis: A graph-based generative model (e.g., a GNN‑VAE) pre-trained on ChEMBL35 and fine‑tuned on the 103 CTF substrates/lattice nodes will generate lattice-node candidates that pass the expert structural and electrochemical filters at least 10 percentage points more often than the best-performing SMILES/SELFIES sequence-based model (REINVENT FT+RL on ChEMBL35) when both are trained under the same computational budget.
